qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Template Injection in element condition rules leads to RCE via disabled Twig sandbox
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause. The finding describes element condition rule elementIds being rendered as Twig through BaseElementSelectConditionRule::getElementIds() and View::renderSandboxedObjectTemplate() while the Twig sandbox is disabled; the advisory describes ElementSearchController::actionSearch() missing unset protection for criteria query properties such as criteria[where] and criteria[orderBy], causing blind SQL injection.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described ElementSearchController SQL injection root cause. The only final qualifying finding is an open-world SSTI/RCE issue in element condition rule Twig rendering.
evidence: The advisory states that ElementSearchController::actionSearch() lacks the unset protection for criteria[where], criteria[orderBy], and other query properties, allowing authenticated control panel users to perform boolean-based blind SQL injection.

ssti-rce-element-condition-twig/vulnerability_report.json describes attacker-controlled elementIds flowing through BaseElementSelectConditionRule::getElementIds() to renderSandboxedObjectTemplate() with enableTwigSandbox=false, enabling Twig template evaluation through condition rules.

The finding mentions POST /admin/actions/element-search/search as one affected entrypoint, but its source-to-sink path and security failure are Twig sandbox bypass/SSTI rather than raw criteria query properties reaching SQL construction.
